summary.oneLine: Sweet-savory ripe plantain slices fried until golden and caramelized; served as a side or snack.
notes: Ripe plantains (yellow with black spots) yield the classic sweet caramelized flavor.
Green plantains can also be fried for a starchy savory version; SSA adaptations include both.
Oil absorption varies by temperature and ripeness; SSA nutrition pipeline should estimate from oil and plantain quantities.
Typically allergen-light; ensure frying oil matches household preferences (e.g., peanut oil restrictions if any).

required.label: Paper towels or rack for draining

ingredients.label: Ripe plantains
notes: Best ripeness: yellow with black spots for sweet caramelization.
For savory fried plantains, use greener plantains (see adaptation).

ingredients.label: Neutral frying oil
notes: Use enough to shallow-fry (about 1/4–1/2 inch depth). Adjust based on pan size.

ingredients.label: Salt
notes: Optional; many households lightly salt after frying.

steps.title: Peel and slice plantains
steps.body: Peel plantains and slice into 1/4–1/2 inch rounds or diagonal slices (bias-cut). Pat dry if very moist.

steps.title: Heat oil for shallow-frying
steps.body: Add oil to skillet (about 1/4–1/2 inch deep). Heat over medium to medium-high until shimmering. Target oil temperature ~350°F / 175°C if using a thermometer. A small piece of plantain should sizzle immediately.

hazards.message: Hot oil can cause serious burns—keep children away, avoid splashing, and use long tongs.

checks: Oil shimmers and sizzles with a test piece

steps.title: Fry until golden (first side)
steps.body: Place plantain slices in a single layer (do not crowd). Fry 2–3 minutes until edges deepen to golden-brown and caramelize.

hazards.message: Oil may splatter—lower heat slightly if vigorous popping occurs.

steps.title: Flip and finish frying
steps.body: Flip slices and fry 2–3 minutes more until golden-brown on both sides and tender throughout. Adjust heat to prevent burning—ripe plantains caramelize quickly.
checks: Tender when pressed lightly with tongs

steps.title: Drain and season
steps.body: Transfer to paper towels or a wire rack to drain for 1–2 minutes. Season lightly with salt (and optional black pepper) while warm. Serve immediately.

suggestions: Serve as a side with jollof rice, stews, and grilled meats.
Serve as a snack with pepper sauce or a simple tomato-onion relish if desired.

reheatNotes: Best eaten fresh. If needed, reheat in a hot skillet or air fryer to restore crisp edges.

substitutions.notes: Bananas can fry but are much softer/sweeter; reduce time and handle gently.
Use a high-smoke-point oil for frying.

equipmentFallbacks.doInstead: Use a fork or slotted spoon carefully; avoid puncturing too much (can increase oil absorption).

allergenConsiderations.notes: Use neutral oil instead of peanut oil when peanut allergen is active.

knownGood: Ripe plantains should caramelize to golden-brown without burning.
Oil should be hot enough to sizzle; too cool makes plantains greasy.
Do not crowd the pan; it drops oil temp and softens texture.

commonIssues.likelyCause: Oil temperature too low or pan overcrowded
commonIssues.fix: Heat oil properly, fry in batches, and drain on a rack/paper towels.

commonIssues.problem: Plantains burned outside but raw inside
commonIssues.likelyCause: Oil temperature too high or slices too thick
commonIssues.fix: Lower heat and slice thinner; fry a bit longer at moderate heat.
Use yellow-with-black-spots plantains for classic sweet fried plantain.
